metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

use_case_name: UC01 — Generate Spec from One-Sentence Idea
initial_conditions: An empty project context exists in the Founder's organization.
At least one LLM Provider is configured and credentialed for the organization.
The Founder has permission to create specs in the organization.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Founder submits a one-sentence product idea

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall authenticate the Founder's session before accepting the idea (CC.R01).
The system shall persist the submitted idea prompt to the empty project context with a stable Project ID.
The system shall initiate a conversational intake session scoped to the Project ID.
The system shall present the first intake question to the Founder within FOUNDER_INTAKE_RESPONSE_BUDGET_MS of receiving the idea prompt.
The system shall classify the idea's domain by invoking a configured LLM Provider with the idea prompt and intake answers.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: LLM Provider returns domain classification and confidence score.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall loop intake questions until the intake completeness threshold INTAKE_COMPLETENESS_THRESHOLD is met.
The system shall assemble an initial scope outline (actors, use cases, constraints) from the intake transcript.
The system shall draft a tech-stack recommendation for each identified subsystem in the scope outline.
The system shall invoke the metric-traceback subroutine for every tech-stack recommendation (<<include>> UC08).
The system shall assemble the enterprise-grade spec bundle (scope, actors, use cases, traced recommendations, constants).
The system shall validate the assembled spec against the spec-shape schema and reject the assembly if validation fails.
The system shall complete the spec generation cycle within SPEC_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_SEC from the first Founder answer.
The system shall persist the validated spec to version-controlled storage with status 'draft' and a commit hash.
The system shall record an audit entry for the spec creation with Founder identity, Project ID, commit hash, and timestamp (CC.R02).

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Product Managers receive notification that the spec is ready for review.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall notify the spec's assigned Product Managers that the spec is ready for review.
The system shall return the persisted spec's URL and commit hash to the Founder.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Founder receives the spec URL and commit hash

ending_conditions: A validated enterprise-grade spec is committed to version-controlled storage with status 'draft' and a commit hash.
Every tech-stack recommendation in the spec is linked to a declared performance metric (availability, throughput, or latency) with a cited source, satisfying TRACEBACK_COVERAGE_PCT.
The assigned Product Managers have been notified that the spec is ready for review.
The Founder holds the spec URL and commit hash.
An audit entry records the spec creation with Founder identity, Project ID, commit hash, and timestamp.
notes: Scope boundary: UC01 covers idea → draft spec only. Spec review is UC03. CLI command emission is UC04. No Module-1 actors are added in this UCBD.
Metric traceback is invoked as <<include>> UC08 on step 12. Every tech-stack recommendation must pass UC08 before the spec assembles successfully.
Latency discipline: FOUNDER_INTAKE_RESPONSE_BUDGET_MS bounds per-turn chat latency (step 5); SPEC_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_SEC bounds end-to-end assembly (step 15).
Cross-cutting: session auth (CC.R01) on every protected action; audit write (CC.R02) on state transitions.
LLM Provider selection is driven by UC13 (deferred). In first-pass v1 we assume the org's configured default provider.
If step 12 (traceback) flags a recommendation below TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE, step 14 validation rejects the spec assembly and surfaces the failure to the Founder for override or refinement.
Write-back semantics (v1 scope): the spec is written to version-controlled storage, not directly back to customer systems. Customer-system integration is UC11.
Degradation: if all configured LLM Providers are unavailable, the system shall fail closed and notify the Founder; no silent degradation.
